title: Moluchia frutescens
intro: Moluchia frutescens, also known as Chile pepper tree, is a shrub-like plant belonging to the family Boraginaceae, native to Mexico and Central America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects that can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential in reducing fever and relieving pain. The bioactive constituents of Moluchia frutescens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Moluchia frutescens is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to harness its medicinal benefits.
taxonomy: Moluchia frutescens is classified within the plant family Boraginaceae. It is a species of flowering plant. The plant is native to South America. Further classification would include the order Boraginales and class Magnoliopsida. More specifically, its full classification is within the kingdom Plantae.
